leadership.name: Dr. Stephen Asatsa
leadership.title: Senior Lecturer & Head of Psychology Department
leadership.bio: Leading research on indigenous psychology, cultural evolution, and decolonizing mental health practices in African contexts. Senior academic with extensive experience in teaching, research, and professional practice.

internationalCollaborators.name: Sheina Lew-Levy, PhD
internationalCollaborators.bio: Assistant professor of Psychology at Durham University whose work explores cultural diversity and the evolution of social learning in childhood, including hunter-gatherer societies.

internationalCollaborators.name: Prof. Dr. Amber Gayle Thalmayer
internationalCollaborators.bio: Leads the Africa Long Life Study across participating countries with a focus on personality, mental health, and culture.

internationalCollaborators.name: Dr. Elizabeth Shino
internationalCollaborators.bio: Research lead for the Namibia arm of the Africa Long Life Study and a featured collaborator from the original site.

internationalCollaborators.name: Prof. Luzelle Naudé
internationalCollaborators.bio: South Africa lead on the Africa Long Life Study and a longstanding academic collaborator.

researchTeam.title: Graduate Research Assistants
researchTeam.description: Dedicated team of graduate students supporting various research projects at the Catholic University of Eastern Africa

members.name: Graduate Research Team
contributions: Data collection for Africa Long Life Study
Community engagement for mourning rituals research

students.title: Student Opportunities
students.description: We welcome graduate and undergraduate students interested in culturally grounded psychology research

opportunities.title: Research Assistantships
opportunities.description: Paid and volunteer positions supporting ongoing research projects

opportunities.title: Graduate Supervision
opportunities.description: Master's and PhD supervision in psychology and related fields

opportunities.title: Internships
